entry.title: Re: Change Company address
entry.content:
Dear customer,

If you need to change the company address of your customer number, could you pls kindly refer to KBA 2602869 and contact your SAP Account Executive?

If you do not know who your SAP Account Executive is, refer to KBA 2626344. 

Then your account manager will work with the SAP Order Management team to change the company address for you.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: 1H2026 Release: A journey to Autonomous Payroll with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Hello Everyone!

To advance our vision of an autonomous payroll, we are introducing four specialized Payroll Agents for the SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) solution in this first half 2026 release.

You can now benefit from automations across 1) payroll and time explanations, 2) alert resolution, 3) rule creation, and 4) data integration. These AI agents detect, explain, and resolve issues before they impact your workforce. They streamline how payroll teams prepare and run payroll by orchestrating checks across time, workflow, and payroll controls.

Below are the key highlights, but be sure to explore the What's New Viewer for the complete release notes and full details on everything we’re delivering.

Payroll Explanation Agent

With this update, the Payroll Explanation Agent connects Payroll and Time sheet data into one unified explanation, giving employees a clear, easy‑to‑understand view of their pay statements and the time data behind them. Employees get crisp, clear and fast answers without relying on HR. This enhances employee experience, builds trust and confidence in payroll accuracy and lowers operational costs. PostNL, one of our customers based in Netherlands, expects 80% cost reduction in handling payroll questions with an AI agent. Additionally, support now extends to Brazil and Canada, building on the 2025 release in the USA, UK, Australia, Mexico, the Netherlands, and Germany.

  • See the Payroll Explanation Agent in action here (video)

 

[Screenshot] Payroll Explanation Agent now integrates Time sheet data and supports Brazil and Canada

Payroll Alert Resolution Agent

This agent streamlines how payroll administrators manage Payroll Control Center (PCC) alerts by turning complex investigations into a guided, intelligent workflow. It explains why alerts occur, identifies the most likely root causes, and recommends prioritized fixes directly within the payroll control center. Administrators can also create new validation rules using natural‑language through a conversational interface.

  • See the Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Payroll Alert Resolution Agent in ECP

Payroll Validation Rule Agent

This rule creation agent introduces a conversational, AI‑assisted way for payroll administrators to build new validation rules directly within the Payroll Control Center. Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins simply describe the rule they need in natural language.

Instead of navigating technical configuration steps, admins can simply describe the rule they need in natural language making the whole process of validation rule creation 50% faster.

[Screenshot] Payroll Validation Rule Agent in ECP

Employee Data Integration Agent

The agent helps payroll administrator resolve payroll replication errors by translating complex technical messages into clear, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central–focused guidance. It identifies impacted EC fields based on ECP mappings and provides step‑by‑step recommendations for fixing data issues. This release addresses manual corrections.

For example, a missing cost center for an employee. Instead of leaving the admin to interpret a technical error message, the agent analyzes organizational data and suggests the most likely cost center—based on where the employee’s peers in the same department and reporting to the same manager are assigned.

The result: cleaner data, fewer payroll interruptions, and far less time spent resolving issues after the fact.

  • See the Employee Data Integration Agent in action here (video)

[Screenshot] Employee Data Integration Agent in ECP

 

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2026 release, including

  • Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities
  • Enhanced Gender Field Replication from Employee Central to Employee Central Payroll
  • Dozens of legal changes for specific country/region (documented in detail in the Regulatory Change Manager)
  • And more

See the What's New Viewer for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2025 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

entry.title: Re: Add New Fields in My Inbox for SAP Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Hello @dbx87 


No, you cannot directly add new fields to the approval details in My Inbox for standard workflow WS00800251 via configuration.

Show existing fields using UI Adaptation
Extend data via CDS View extension + annotations
Use BAdI  workflow extension to pass additional data

Not possible - Adding completely new custom fields in My Inbox UI without backend/service extension


Extend the OData service (via CDS) + expose fields → then adapt UI


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: CLM Lease-out revenue postings on cost center / wbs-e...
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 ,

Thanks for your very extensive answer. Unfortunately, it is not the answer we hope to receive. I am aware that we can use cost centers and wbs-elements. Both are meant to post expenses and not revenues.

One company code of ours is leasing out real estate, meaning this is the revenue driver. It would be strange to post it as negative costs on a cost center or create a project structure for these postings. I understand the reasoning from a lease-in point of view, but not really for lease-out. If it was a lease-out beneath a lease-in it would also make sense.

Looking back to RE-FX the contract and master data objects where the cost objects. Meaning that we were able to create a P&L on these objects. In CLM the master data objects have disappeared or, in case of usable/rentable objects, also assigned to a cost center / wbs-element. In the past RE-FX was not linked to the SD functionality or profitability analysis. However, then we could use the RE-FX objects/contracts.

If we go for revenue postings on the profit center, we must use a dummy cost center. This does not feel correct. We cannot use an actual revenue cost element, but the GL account must still be marked as cost element.

We are searching for a future proof solution where we can use the lease-out contracts to generate revenue and post these on revenue elements with a correct assignment for managerial reporting. It would make sense to move towards Profitability Analysis because we want to use additional dimensions as well.

I hope that SAP will also read this thread, but perhaps I will create an influence request for it.

Best regards, Jeroen

entry.title: custom field to the business volume selection criteria in Condition Contracts - Sales Rebates
entry.content:
Hello community

We are working with the scope item Sales Rebate Processing (1B6) in sap public cloud.

We have the following questions:
- Is it possible to add a custom field to the business volume selection criteria in the "Manage Condition Contracts - Sales Rebates" app?

- We have seen in the SAP documentation that it is possible to add custom fields to the condition contract at the header level using the "Settlement Management: Condition Contract" context. However, this custom field does not appear in business volume selection criteria

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales 

Thank you, best regards.

entry.title: Enhancing Enterprise Applications: AI-powered User Experience with SAP CAP AI Plugin
entry.content:
In today’s world, users expect their applications to smartly reduce their manual effort and help them complete their tasks quickly.

And enterprise applications are no exception. Imagine looking at a huge, blank form that you must fill out to complete a mundane but mandatory task at the office. You can practically feel the energy drain out of your body as the form stares back at you. Suddenly, a coffee break looks enticing 🙂

But what if your application could generate recommendations based on the data available in the system, and dramatically cut your effort to just eyeballing the correctness of most of the fields, only requiring you to fill in a few?

While this may be on every user’s wish list, implementing these AI-driven smart features in existing (or even new) applications can require a lot of development effort.

At SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP), we believe in reducing boilerplate code and letting developers focus on intent. Hence, in the April release of CAP, we have released the SAP CAP AI plugin for node.js. (Watch this space for the Java version, which will be released soon).

This plugin will enable existing and new CAP applications to use recommendations generated by the RPT-1 Generative AI model provided by SAP AI Core. The recommendations will then be pre-filled in the UI of the application. All this is out-of-the-box, without writing a single line of code.

The CAP AI plugin does three main things:

  1. Simplifies AI Core configuration
  2. Provide recommendations on the UI coming from RPT-1
  3. Inherits the best of CAP

Simplifying AI Core configuration –

Admin tasks such as creating a new SAP AI Core resource group per tenant and creating an RPT-1 deployment are automated using the “AICore” service created by the plugin.

Recommendations in the UI application –

Any entity with a value help (@Common.ValueList on the property or @CDS.odata.valuelist on the association target) will be used by the plugin to show the recommendations.

 

entity Books {
  key ID : Integer;
  title  : String(111);
  descr  : String(1111);
  genre : Association to one Genres;
  status : Association to one Status;
}
annotate Genres with @cds.odata.valuelist;
annotate Books with {
    status @Common.ValueList : {
        CollectionPath : 'Status',
        Parameters: [
            {
                $Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut'
                ValueListProperty : 'code',
                LocalDataProperty : status_code
            }
        ]
    }
}

 

 

The best of CAP

All the battle-tested enterprise readiness of CAP is inherently part of the plugin. It also supports multi-tenancy, and local testing without an SAP AI Core instance.

You can read more about the plugin here - https://github.com/cap-js/ai

We are excited to have you try out this plugin.  If you have any feature requests or suggestions, please feel free to reach out to us.

Till then, happy coding!

entry.title: Re: ECC Transaction/Fiori app to be used for Mass deletio...
entry.content:
Hello, 

As observed, requisition items cannot be set for deletion in the mass changes app. Also, deletion indicator is not available in MEMASSRQ. In order to set the EBAN-LOEKZ flag, please use BAPI_PR_CHANGE calling it for all requisitions you need to change. This is documented in the following KBA:

2666219 - Deletion flag indicator can not be used with MEMASSRQ - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA.

Regards, 

 

Maria

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After...
entry.content:
Hi James,

Adding a user in the Users section of the BTP Cockpit does not actually create a new user. It is just an entry mapping 'some user' with their roles. The actual user creation (and password maintenance) happens in the Identity Provider linked to your BTP sub account (see Trust Configuration). In case you are using the 'Default Identity Provider' (this would use your S-user or P-user created by SAP) try and log on the IdP directly via https://accounts.sap.com/ui/protected/profilemanagement. In case you are using a custom Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant, first create a user in the IAS via https://your-tenant-id.accounts.ondemand.com/admin and then the mapping in the BTP Cockpit will take effect.

entry.title: Beyond the Bot: Why Utilities Need “Agentic” AI on SAP BTP to Solve the Grid Complexity Crisis
entry.content:
Beyond the Bot: Why Utilities Need “Agentic” AI on SAP BTP to Solve the Grid Complexity Crisis

 

The Grid Complexity Wall

The utility industry is hitting a wall—and it’s not a small one.

For decades, power grids were predictable. Energy flowed in one direction: from generation to transmission to distribution to the customer. Systems like SAP IS-U were designed around this linear model. Billing cycles were stable, meter roads were periodic, and exceptions were manageable.

That world no longer exists.

Now things change, today’s grid is dynamic, decentralized, and highly unpredictable. Electric vehicles charge at random times. Rooftop solar pushes power back into the grid. Heat pumps create new consumption patterns. Customers are no longer just consumers, they are producers.

This shift has introduced a level of complexity that traditional systems were never designed to handle.

Due to this now?

Utilities are now overwhelmed with:

  • Billing exceptions.
  • Meter data inconsistencies.
  • Unexpected usage of spikes.
  • Data reconciliation issues across systems .

 

Many organizations tried to solve this with automation, especially RPA. But here’s the reality: bots don’t solve complexity. They only automate repetition.

 

A bot can follow a script. It cannot understand context, and that’s exactly where the problem lies.

To manage the “messy grid” of today, utilities need to move beyond deterministic automation. They need systems that can interpret, decide, and act.

This is where Agentic AI comes in.

 

Why a “Messy Core” Kills “AI”

Before we talk about AI, we need to talk about architecture. One of the biggest misconceptions is that you can simply “add AI” on top of an existing SAP landscape. AI is only as effective as the environment it operates in.

Agentic AI systems depend on something critical: observability.

They need to understand:

  • What rules exist.
  • How decisions are made.
  • Where do the data come from.
  • What events are happening in real time.

If your core SAP system is filled with deeply embedded custom logic—user exits, enhancements, Z-programs—then that logic is invisible to AI.

 

It becomes a black box and if AI cannot “see” the rules, it cannot reason.

This is why Clean Core is not just an SAP best practice anymore—it is a foundational requirement for AI.

A clean core ensures:

  • Business logic is transparent.
  • Processes are standardized.
  • Extensions are externalized and visible.

 

This is where SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) plays a key role. A simple way to think about it:

  • S/4HANA is the system that executes transactions reliably .
  • BTP is the layer where intelligence, decisions, and adaptability live

If the core is the engine, BTP is the brain. Without that separation, AI cannot function effectively.

Architecture View: Clean Core + Agentic AI on BTP

 

 

Agentic AI vs. Traditional RPA

Let’s make this real with a common utility scenario.

Scenario: High Usage Billing Dispute

A customer receives a bill that is significantly higher than expected and raises a dispute.

What happened today with RPA?

  • The system flags the anomaly
  • A bot may stop the invoice or trigger a workflow
  • The case is routed to a human agent
  • Investigation is manual, time-consuming, and inconsistent

The bot did its job—but only partially. It identified the issue but could not resolve it.

Now imagine the same scenario with Agentic AI

An AI agent doesn’t just flag—it investigates.

It can:

  • Analyze historical consumption patterns
  • Cross-check weather data for abnormal usage
  • Validate meter health from AMI systems
  • Review distributed energy resource activity (like solar export)
  • Identify whether the issue is behavioral, environmental, or technical

Then it can act:

  • Determine probable root cause
  • Trigger a field service request if needed
  • Adjust billing or flag for human approval
  • Log the entire decision process for audit

The key difference is simple:

Bots automate steps.
Agents handle outcomes.

This shift—from execution to reasoning—is what makes Agentic AI fundamentally different.

The Roadmap to Autonomous Utility

Moving to this model doesn’t happen overnight. But there is a clear path forward.

  1. Event-Driven Foundations

Traditional SAP systems operate in batches. That doesn’t work in real-time grid.

Utilities need to adopt event-driven architecture using services like SAP Event Mesh.

This allows systems to:

  • React instantly to meter events
  • Trigger workflows in real time
  • Enable AI agents to act as events occur

Without events, there is no real-time intelligence.

  1. Externalized Logic

One of the biggest shifts is moving business logic out of the core.

This includes:

  • Billing rules
  • Validation logic
  • Regulatory checks
  • Exception handling

Using CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) on BTP, this logic becomes:

  • Modular
  • Transparent
  • Easier to maintain
  • Accessible to AI systems

This is critical because AI needs structured, visible logic to operate effectively.

  1. Semantic Data Layer

AI is only as good as the data it uses.

Utilities typically have fragmented data across:

  • SAP systems
  • GIS platforms
  • AMI systems
  • Customer platforms

A semantic layer helps unify this data into a consistent model.

This ensures:

  • One version of truth
  • Better context for AI decisions
  • Reduced ambiguity

Without this, even the most advanced AI will produce unreliable results.

Governance: The Guardrails of Agentic AI

With great intelligence comes great responsibility, especially in a regulated industry like utilities.

You cannot allow AI to operate without control.

Key concerns include:

  • Regulatory compliance
  • Billing accuracy
  • Auditability
  • Customer trust

The answer is not to limit AI—but to govern it properly.

This includes:

  • Human-in-the-loop checkpoints for critical decisions
  • Clear approval workflows
  • Full traceability of AI decisions
  • Strong grounding using SAP AI Core

Governance ensures that AI operates within defined boundaries.

In fact, Clean Core governance becomes even more important in an AI-driven world. It ensures that all decisions—whether human or machine—follow a consistent and compliant path.

Conclusion: Survival of the Modern Grid

Utilities today are at a turning point.

The complexity of the grid is not going away. It will only increase.

Organizations that continue to rely on heavy core customization are creating a long-term problem. They are building systems that are harder to adapt, harder to scale, and impossible for AI to understand.

In many ways, this becomes a “digital anchor”—holding them back while the industry moves forward.

On the other hand, utilities that embrace:

  • Clean Core principles
  • BTP as an innovation platform
  • Event-driven architecture
  • Agentic AI

will be in a position to not just manage complexity—but to turn it into an advantage.

The journey doesn’t need to start big.

Start small.

Pick one high-volume exception process—billing disputes, meter validation, or service orders. Externalize the logic. Build it on BTP. Introduce intelligence step by step.

That’s how transformation begins.

Because the future utility is not just automated.

It is autonomous, adaptive, and intelligent.

And that future starts now.

entry.title: Re: Custom Fields Failing to Influence Procurement Pricin...
entry.content:
Good day @alan_tsai 

Thank you for your question.

The thing to note is that you are creating custom fields on the PO item and maintaining condition records with those custom fields doe not automatically make those fields available in the procurement pricing communication structure.

There is no configuration path in the system where you can manually map PO item custom fields to KOMP or to a procurement pricing communication entity. KBA 3687197 that limitation.

This is why you see the condition type PPR0 in pricing analysis, but the custom field values appear as initial or Not Found. The condition access can technically exist, but the pricing engine does not receive the custom field value in the pricing communication layer. Therefore, the system cannot use that custom field value to determine the correct condition record. This basically means:

  • The custom fields can exist on the PO item.
  • The custom fields can be visible in the PO UI if you enable it correctly.
  • The condition type PPR0 can still be processed.
  • However, the custom PO item fields are not automatically passed into procurement pricing comms.
  • There is no released config activity to map those fields manually to KOMP.
  • The BAdI MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL can trigger or control pricing determination, but it does not create a missing KOMP mapping by itself.
  • If the pricing analysis shows the custom fields as initial, the condition record cannot be found based on those fields.

See KBA 3580006 for details on enhancement option for purchase oder.

So to summarise:

  • In the standard released scope, the PO item custom fields cannot be enabled in the way you are trying to use them.
  • Implementing MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL does not solve the missing field value issue by itself.
  • PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT can only be used based on the requirement. See KBA 2756560 and KBA 3687197 for more insights.

My recommended approach is not to use unsupported PO item custom fields directly in the PPR0 access sequence. Instead, consider using one of these options:

  1. Use standard pricing fields wherever possible. Make sure you redesign the access sequence around released standard fields. (most appropriate).
  2. Use MM_PUR_S4_PO_DEFINE_CNDNCTRL only to trigger redetermination. The BAdI can help with when pricing runs, not with making the missing field available in KOMP.
  3. Use alternative condition amount calculation only when the values you need are available. If your requirement is not strict condition record determination, but price calculation based on custom logic, review the purchase order price enhancement approach in KBA 3580006:
    1. Create a custom logic for PRCG_DOC_ITEM_CONDITION_AMOUNT.
    2. Create a calculation routing in SSCUI 102430.
    3. Copy or adapt the pricing procedure in SSUI 101095.
    4. Assign the routine to the condition type in the pricing procedure and test the PO pricing result.

This is only valid if the values needed for the calculation are available in the BAdI context. If your PO item custom fields are not available there, this will not resolve the same root issue.

 

You may also consider raising a feature request if the custom fields must be access sequence fields.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: System cannot be deleted
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know before deleting a on-prem system, you have to close all the open and draft cases under the system to be deleted.

So pls kindly close all those open cases first, then try to delete the systems again per KBA 1843303 - How to delete On-premise systems - SAP for Me.

In case there is no any open cases under the system but the system still cannot be deleted, feel free to contact us by creating a case using component XX-SER-FORME.

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: BTP User Cannot Receive “Forgot Password” Email After...
entry.content:
Forgot password is functionality is from IAS. For this the user should already be created/onboarded in IAS. When you create an IAS tenant, a user with the same email address will be automatically onboarded in IAS. 

Adding a user in subaccount is only creating a shadow user record that can be used for assigning role collections in the subaccount. It does not create a user in connected IAS.

You need to onboard the user in IAS.

entry.title: Re: Add value to Sales Order header Usage dropdown; confi...
entry.content:
Dear @harshad_p,

In Public Cloud the Sales Order header Usage dropdown cannot be extended by customer configuration. There is no SSCUI, CBC configuration activity or customer-maintainable table available to add new values to this standard dropdown

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3330346

Recommended workaround

Use a custom field at Sales Document/Sales Order header level, preferably with a fixed value help/code list, if the business needs an additional classification value. SAP Public Cloud extensibility content confirms that custom fields can be added to sales documents and propagated through related sales/billing scenarios where supported

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/applying-localization-as-a-self-service-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/adding-custom-fields-to-sales-documents

Summary:

The standard Usage dropdown at Sales Order header level is not extendable by configuration in Public Cloud. Use a clean-core custom field/code list instead or raise an SAP influence request

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2963059

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Help Keep SAP IBP #1: Your Product Experience Matters
entry.content:
Product Experience Survey - Help Us Make SAP IBP Work Better for You

 When the Product Experience Survey appears in your SAP IBP application, it’s your chance to share what you like, what’s working well and what isn’t. 

We’re looking to understand where you struggle in your daily work—those tasks that take longer than they should or cause friction. We also want to know which features truly make your life easier and work well for you, plus how we can improve your overall experience with SAP IBP applications. Here is a link to a video showing you how to launch the PX Survey in SAP IBP. 

How the PX Survey Works

The PX survey appears directly in your app or can be started manually when you have feedback to share. Your responses are linked to the specific application you’re using, so our development teams get targeted, actionable insights.

 

Where to Find It:

For the Fiori Launchpad you’ll find the survey in the upper right corner using the speech bubble with a smiley icon.

Alternatively you can also use the SAP IBP add-in for Microsoft Excel:

In the IBP Menu goto About -> give Feedback

What’s Included

The survey includes a Product Satisfaction score, Usefulness and Ease of Use ratings, and a comment field for additional details.

 

 

Getting the Most from Your Feedback

Do:

  • Respond when the survey appears—it only takes a moment
  • Focus your comments on the specific app mentioned

Don’t:

  • Include personal information in comments
  • Leave generic responses—specific feedback is more helpful

 

Your Input Makes a Difference

Your feedback helps us understand what’s working and what needs attention. When you take a moment to respond, you’re helping improve the daily experience for yourself and other users.

The next time you see the PX survey, consider sharing your thoughts—it’s one of the most direct ways to influence how SAP IBP evolves.

Reference:

  • Video showing you how to launch the PX Survey in SAP IBP

entry.title: Changing the shipping point after integration with TM
entry.content:
Hello everybody, Ilia is here.

I'm looking for solution advice for a given business process and system landscape. I would be grateful to hear your comments:

Process:

We are currently in an OrderToCash process involving the following SAP modules: SD, LE, TM and EWM.
High level business process looks like this:
At regular intervals, the customer submits a request for the delivery of specific materials by a specific date. The request is processed outside SAP, but information regarding when and in what quantity the customer expects delivery is received in SAP via an interface.
Next, we need to locate the material in the warehouse, prepare it for transport and load it onto the scheduled truck.
Logistically, the process ends once the vehicle has left the company premises. Subsequent financial processes have been omitted for the sake of simplicity.

There is a logistical feature to the process:

Sometimes large quantities of materials are produced, and any surplus is then transported to an external warehouse.

When preparing the transport planning, the planner assesses the stock levels of materials at the on-site warehouse and at the external warehouse. If there are sufficient quantities of materials at the external warehouse to fulfil the delivery, it will be dispatched from there.

 System reflection of the process:

We are working on a brownfield implementation with existing sales and planning processes that cannot be changed on the fly. We are in ASR context.

Contracts have been concluded with customers, and the aforementioned recurring delivery requests are recorded in sales via Sales Agreements. When a customer requests a new delivery, a new schedule line is created in the Sales Agreement.


This part of the process already exists and cannot be changed at present.

Another point: each sales agreement also implies the delivery of only one material. By default, a schedule line in a Sales Agreement is assigned to a shipping point. In our case, this is the shipping point of our factory rather than an external warehouse.
Consequently, after a schedule line is created, it is integrated into the Transport Management (TM) module, where the freight unit inherits the shipping point from the schedule line.
An outbound delivery will be created from the TM module, incorporating the material quantity, shipping point, and consignee. 
The challenge we faced: we would like to be able to change the shipping point during transport planning to dispatch goods from a different origin location. -> an external warehouse in our example. Ideally, using standard functions, but personally, I have little faith that this is possible.

What we have already tried and what can be excluded:

1. Creating deliveries from LE and changing the shipping point there, before distribution to TM. Copy control between SA and Delivery does not allow this.
2. Changing the Sales Agreement via the transport planner is not permitted by organisational policies.
3. Creating individual sales orders with a specified shipping point is also not permitted for organisational reasons.

We will most likely have to develop a custom solution, but I decided to seek advice to see if there might be a less obvious solution to the problem. Thank you for your replies!

entry.title: Re: Pending Transport Requests from ECC to S/4HANA – Which Tool to Use?
entry.content:
Transport requests are typically not transferred directly from ECC to S/4HANA after migration, as objects often require adjustment or re-creation due to data model and simplification changes.
Typically, developers/functional teams handle the content, while Basis manages the transport routes and import process.

entry.title: How We Built ASTRA Joule Agent for Automation Testing Solution for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
entry.content:
Introduction

In my experience working on multiple SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementations, one phase that consistently stands out as challenging is testing. No matter how well the system is designed, testing often becomes time-consuming and difficult to manage—especially when dealing with multiple modules like Employee Central, Payroll, and integrations.

During several projects, I observed that teams were spending a significant amount of effort on repetitive manual validation, coordination across systems, and analyzing failures. While automation tools helped to some extent, they still required heavy setup and maintenance.

This led us to explore how we could make testing more efficient and intelligent using SAP BTP and AI capabilities. That’s how we started working on what we now call the ASTRA Joule HR Agent—a solution aimed at simplifying and improving the testing experience.

The Challenge We Faced

From a practical standpoint, testing in SAP SuccessFactors is not just about executing scripts—it involves validating complete business processes and ensuring data consistency across systems.

Some of the common challenges we encountered were:

  • Handling large regression cycles with hundreds of scenarios
  • Managing complex and realistic test data
  • Difficulty in identifying the root cause of failures
  • Constant switching between multiple tools (SuccessFactors, test tools, Jira, etc.)

These issues often slowed down release cycles and required significant manual effort.

Our Approach – ASTRA Joule HR Agent

To address these gaps, we built ASTRA as a centralized, intelligent powerhouse on SAP Business Technology Platform. We leveraged SAP Business AI through Joule skills to create a conversational, "human-centric" testing experience.

Instead of navigating complex menus, users can interact with ASTRA through SAP Build Work Zone. By integrating Joule, we’ve made it possible to manage tests, gain insights into failures, and even trigger executions using natural language.

Key Technical Highlights:

  • Prebuilt Library: We launched with 926+ automation scripts aligned with SAP Best Practices.

  • Full-Stack Integration: Built using the SAP Cloud Application Programming  model, ensuring seamless OData connectivity with SuccessFactors and S/4HANA.

  • AI Orchestration: Using Joule to provide smarter interactions and faster insights into test logs.

Benefits

1. Extensive Module Coverage

ASTRA doesn’t just "test"; it validates the entire HCM lifecycle. This includes Employee Central, Recruiting, Onboarding, Benefits, Time Management, and Performance & Goals. It even handles custom Extension Center objects, ensuring your unique business logic is never left unchecked.

 

 

2. End-to-End & Integration Testing - Enables both functional and integration testing across SAP SuccessFactors, SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and external systems—helping ensure full end-to-end process validation.

 

 

 

 

 

 

3. AI-Powered Insights (Joule Skills)

By utilizing SAP Business AI, ASTRA helps users understand why a test failed without digging through raw logs. Joule assists in identifying failed scenarios and provides intelligent summaries, drastically reducing investigation time.

 

 

 

 

4. Dynamic, Input-Driven Execution

Efficiency is key. ASTRA allows users to enter specific Entity IDs to run focused tests for individual records. The system dynamically filters and executes only the relevant test cases based on your input data.

 

 

 

5. Automated Documentation & Defect Management

  • Traceability: Every execution generates a PDF-based result document for step-by-step audit trails.

  • Jira Integration: Failed scenarios automatically trigger ticket creation in JIRA, ensuring the engineering team is notified in real-time.

 

 

 

6. Joule-Powered Assistance with SAP Business AI (Joule Skills)

  • Uses SAP Business AI through Joule skills to enhance test execution and analysis.
  • Provides intelligent assistance to help users understand test results and failures faster.
  • Helps identify failed scenarios and related logs without manual analysis.
  • Improves overall testing efficiency by reducing time spent on investigation and coordination.

 

 

Business Value

From what I’ve observed while working with this approach, the impact is quite practical and noticeable in day-to-day project execution:

  • Faster Release Cycles: With better visibility into test results and fewer manual dependencies, we were able to move through release cycles with more confidence and less delay.
  • Reduced Manual Effort: A significant portion of repetitive validation was minimized, which allowed both functional and technical teams to spend more time on critical tasks rather than regression testing.
  • Improved Data Reliability: By validating end-to-end scenarios with realistic test data, we could identify issues earlier and avoid unexpected problems during go-live.
  • Better Visibility for Stakeholders: Having centralized dashboards within SAP Build Work Zone made it easier for stakeholders to track progress, understand results, and make informed decisions without relying on multiple tools.

Conclusion

Based on my experience, testing in SAP SuccessFactors projects often becomes a coordination-heavy activity. What worked well for us with this approach was shifting the focus from managing test execution to actually improving system quality.

By leveraging capabilities available on SAP Business Technology Platform along with Joule, we were able to make testing more streamlined and easier to manage.

The ASTRA Joule HR Agent, as we implemented it, is not just about automation—it’s about making testing more meaningful, reducing effort, and helping teams deliver stable and reliable HR solutions more consistently.

entry.title: Re: In the interface for creating a purchase order, is th...
entry.content:
Hello @JUNJUNPAN 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description, the standard purchase order interface doe not necessarily expose the same account assignment fields that are available in the manual Fiori or GUI based Po screen.

See Account Assignment in Purchase Order for more details on the objects to which the PO cost is assigned.

The profitability segment itself is a CO-PA object. See KBA 2325618 for more details.

Note, in a clean-core setup, you should not rely on unreleased technical fields such as EKNN-PAOBJNR for interface posting. See KBA 3235677 for more details on profitability segmentation derivation and its dependency.

My recommendation is as follows:

  • Pass supported account assignment objects through the PO interface.
  • If your business needs customer, project, profit center, or profitability related dimensions in the accounting document, maintain the derivation logic in the finance side where supported. For example, use:
    • Account assignment category in the PO
    • WBS element or cost objects assignment
    • Profit center derived from the account assignment object
    • Substitution or validation rules where the scenario supports them

See KBA 3235677 for more details on automatic derivation details.

  • Use the manual PO field only where the process is manual. If your PO is created through the interface, the design should be based on the fields exposed by the released standard PO API

Alternatively, you can consider raising a feature request if direct profitability segment input is mandatory via Influence Opportunity Homepage - Customer Influence.

To summarise,

  • You next step should be to design the integration around the supported account assignment fields in the purchase order interface and,
  • Let the accounting document derive the required analytical dimension during follow-on posting.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Info-session: Discover Sports Analytics education with NHL® & SAP - Session on May 27
entry.content:
Empower your students with data literacy using real-world NHL® game data and SAP Analytics Cloud. No cost, no complex setup, just powerful learning.

Why Educators Love It:

  • Real Data: Over 1 million data points per game.
  • Ready-to-Teach: Immersive scenarios designed for immediate classroom use.
  • Industry Tools: Students build skills in SAP Analytics Cloud, used by the world's top companies.

For Educators

Register for a free instructor account, or join a live info-session, visiting BusinessBuilders or directly via the following links: 

- Infosession on 27 May: Microsoft Virtual Events Powered by Teams

- Infosession on 28 May: Microsoft Virtual Events Powered by Teams

 

Info-session: Discover Sports Analytics education with NHL® & SAP

We invite you to an info-session to dive into the future of sports analytics education.

Bring hockey into your classroom and connect passion for sports with learning. All for free.

Developed by HEC Montréal in collaboration with SAP and the NHL®, The Hockey Analyst is a new Business Builders data analytics game that gives students hands-on experience with real NHL® game data. Using SAP Analytics Cloud, students take on the role of hockey analysts and work with authentic shot, pass, and performance data. Along the way, they learn to interpret insights, question data, tell compelling data stories while understanding what drives goal scoring using professional-grade analytics software used across industries.

Whether you teach business, analytics, information system, sport management, bachelor's or master's programs. The Hockey Analyst helps you elevate your teaching impact and boost classroom engagement.

Info-session agenda

  • Introduction to The Hockey Analyst
  • Benefits for you as an educator and for your students
  • Gameplay
  • Q&A session with the project leads and HEC Montréal

Webinar details

  • Date/Time: Thursday, May 27 at 5:30 PM CET
  • Duration: 75 minutes

Don't miss this opportunity to be among the first to bring this cutting-edge experience into your classroom.

Important:

  • This session is for educators only.
  • Webinar registration: Please register with your university e-mail address and include the name of your institutions.
  • Complete your Business Builders registration by May 27: Click on "register for instructor account" and sign up using your university email and institution name. This is required to actively participate (play) during the session. After this date, you can still attend the session, but you won't be able to play.
  • Questions: please reach out to universityalliances@sap.com

We look forward to sharing this innovative game with you.

Sincerely,

Academic Partnerships, SAP SE

entry.title: Re: Default Billing Settings in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @muhammad_akbar481,

The Billing Settings in app Create Billing Documents F0798 are maintained directly in the app by the billing user. There is no Public Cloud CBC/SSCUI configuration step available to centrally default these switches for all users before go-live

When option Set billing date and type before billing = ON is active the system displays a popup during billing creation where the user can enter the Billing Type and Billing Date. SAP confirms that when this flag is turned on the popup is shown and the first default value is blank if another billing document is created in the same session the previously entered Billing Type and Billing Date can be proposed again

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003156070

Alternative if the billing date should be determined automatically

If the business requirement is to determine the billing date automatically by rule, instead of relying on each user to enter it manually the clean-core Public Cloud approach is to use billing extensibility, specifically Badi SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER / Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents. SAP documents this Badi for controlling how data is copied from reference documents, such as sales orders, to billing documents

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/3714184b72b54dad819a7c398a701152.html

SAP KBA 2892513 and 3539425 is Public Cloud-specific and confirms that custom logic in SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER can be used to set the billing document date for example to set it equal to the billing creation date

More details below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002892513

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003539425

Summary:

In Public Cloud the F0798 Billing Settings cannot be centrally defaulted for all users through CBC/SSCUI. Each billing user maintains the setting in the app or the billing date should be derived automatically through the released billing Badi if a system-controlled rule is required

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Demystifying SAP Transportation Management Freight Cost Allocation (FCA) in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Many times I get asked how the freight cost allocation needs to be configured and what are the correct  Use Case Types, Settlement Process Types, Pricing Procedures etc. in SAP TM and specially from those customers who have moved from LE-TRA or SAP TM 9.5 or who have upgraded from SAP S/4HANA lower releases to latest SAP S/4HANA 2025, dont understand what has changed in this process. In this blog I try to clarify these to best of my ability and experience.

 

Overview — The Three Phases of FCA Document Type Evolution

 

SAP Transportation Management's Freight Cost Allocation (FCA) business process — business process category 2FCA — has evolved through three distinct phases. Each phase introduced different settlement document types configured in table TMFK, driven by tighter integration with S/4HANA's Financial Accounting and Material Ledger. A critical technical dimension of this evolution — the USE_CASE field in view WZRE_V_DATA — acts as the routing key that determines how Settlement Management processes and posts each document.

Phase 1 — Legacy

Settlement document types: 1600 & 1601

Cost Distribution on G/L - TM
USE_CASE=30 · FR2 account key
Generic settlement, not FCA-branded

Phase 2 — First FCA Types

Settlement document types: 2TM1 & 2TM2

Freight Cost Allocation (Expense)
USE_CASE=31 · AXX003 pricing
TM1 account key · FCA BO introduced

Phase 3 — ML-Account FCA

Settlement document types: 2TM3 & 2TM4

Freight Cost Allocation (ML-Account)
USE_CASE=30 · WT0001 pricing
TM1 account key · ML integration

Current Recommendation

Settlement document types: 2TM3 / 2TM4

Preferred in S/4HANA with mandatory ML actual costing (CKMLCP)

 

Phase-by-Phase Analysis

 

Phase 1: Document Types 1600 & 1601 — "Cost Distribution on G/L"

Concept: The original approach treated freight cost allocation as a generic G/L cost distribution. There was no dedicated "FCA" identity — 1600/1601 used the same settlement infrastructure as other non-FCA settlement processes. The process type WRART=1600 maps directly to LFART=1600 (same value), indicating an early, undifferentiated design with no separation between process definition and document type.

USE_CASE=30 / WT0001: The standard TM settlement pricing procedure WT0001 was used. No FCA-specific pricing conditions existed. The G/L account posting was driven by the generic FR2 account key (Freight General Clearing).

RFBFK=X on 1600: The invoice type had the FI posting block active, requiring a manual release before FI documents were generated. This was intentional to allow review before financial posting.

KTOSL_VE=FR2: A generic freight clearing account key — not TM-specific. Linked to standard freight G/L accounts in account determination, shared across multiple settlement scenarios.

AGENCY_DOC_CAT=2 (Debit): The document was modeled as a debit posting (outgoing cost). Later types switched to AGENCY_DOC_CAT=1 (credit/invoice model), reflecting a modernized document flow design.

Limitations: (1) No FCA identity — LFART_TYPE blank, SEMANTIC_TYPE=0000. (2) No distinction between ML-based and expense-based scenarios. (3) Generic FR2 account key prevents TM-specific G/L account differentiation.

Phase 2: Document Types 2TM1 & 2TM2 — "First Proper FCA Types (Expense)"

Major Advancement: Introduction of proper FCA identity and a dedicated expense posting path. The new WRART=2TME is distinct from LFART=2TM1, cleanly separating process definition from document type.

USE_CASE=31 (New Expense Path!): A dedicated FCA expense pricing procedure AXX003 was introduced. This is the most significant change in Phase 2: AXX003 contains condition types designed to post freight costs as operating expenses to P&L accounts, cost centers, profit centers, or internal orders — not to balance sheet or inventory accounts.

LFART_TYPE=B: The FCA-specific variant indicator was introduced. This enables variant-based processing rules in Settlement Management specific to the FCA scenario.

SEMANTIC_TYPE=0013: FCA-specific semantic rules were activated. This enables FCA-specific business logic, validations, and reporting capabilities that do not apply to generic settlement processes.

RFBFK=blank: The FI posting block was removed for 2TM1. Settlement documents post directly to FI without manual release, accelerating the expense allocation cycle.

Account Key TM1: A new TM-specific clearing account key (TM1) replaced FR2, enabling dedicated G/L account assignment exclusively for TM FCA postings.

Why 2TM1/2TM2 were insufficient for ML scenarios: The AXX003 pricing procedure and USE_CASE=31 route costs to expense accounts. In S/4HANA environments where Material Ledger actual costing (CKMLCP) is active, freight costs must flow through ML-relevant accounts — not be posted as period expenses. 2TM1/2TM2 could not satisfy this ML integration requirement. Additionally, the missing RFBFK block meant FI was posted immediately without the controlled period-end timing that ML costing requires.

Phase 3: Document Types 2TM3 & 2TM4 — "ML-Account FCA (Current Preferred)"

Purpose: Complete the FCA picture by providing dedicated document types for the Material Ledger integration scenario — mandatory in most S/4HANA production implementations.

USE_CASE=30 (Returns to ML/GL path): The 2TM3/2TM4 types deliberately revert to USE_CASE=30 and pricing procedure WT0001. WT0001 contains the condition types needed to post to ML-relevant G/L accounts. The difference from old 1600 is the new TM1 account key, new WRART (2TMM vs 1600), and AGENCY_DOC_CAT=1.

RFBFK=X reinstated on 2TM3: The posting block is re-introduced for the invoice type. This is critical for ML integration: the settlement document is created and held, allowing the ML actual costing run (CKMLCP) to capture and process it at the correct time during period-end close. The reversal type (2TM4) correctly has no block.

LFART_TYPE=B maintained: The FCA variant designation is preserved, consistent with all post-Phase-1 types.

SEMANTIC_TYPE=0000 (Generic): Unlike 2TM1/2TM2, the ML-Account types do not use FCA-specific semantic overrides. The ML posting path follows standard settlement rules. The account determination (TM1 key) ensures correctness without requiring FCA semantic business logic.

Expanded REF_DOC_TYPE coverage: REF_DOC_TYPE 12 (Sales Order / Contract), 13 (Delivery) , 15 (Inbound Delivery) , and 17 (Purchase Order / Contract)  are supported — the same as old 1600 — giving full coverage (compared to the narrower 12 (Sales Order) / 13 (Delivery)  in Phase 2).

 

When to Use Which Document Type

 

Scenario

Document Type

USE_CASE

Rationale

S/4HANA with Material Ledger actual costing active (CKMLCP mandatory)

2TM3 / 2TM4

30

WT0001 pricing → ML account posting; RFBFK=X ensures correct CKMLCP period-end timing; full REF_DOC_TYPE coverage

Freight costs allocated as direct operating expenses (cost centers, P&L, profit segments)

2TM1 / 2TM2

31

AXX003 pricing → expense posting; direct FI transfer

Legacy systems or configurations without FCA-specific requirements

1600 / 1601 (deprecated)

30

Still functional; lacks FCA identity, TM-specific account key.

Enjoy reading !!!

entry.title: Re: ALM Health Monitoring With Number Ranges Warning
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Can you please attach the screenshot again?
Please note that the "Number range" metric lists the top 100 number ranges in the system. For each number range, the current number of entries and the maximum number of entries is shown. Based on this, the usage in percent is calculated.

The rating is based on the usage of the number range. It is set to "Warning" when the usage exceeds the value maintained in the field PERCENTAGE of the number range definition table TNRO.  Can you please check the field PERCENTAGE of the affected Number range in table TNRO?

In addition, please check the SP level of ST-PI on this ABAP system and ensure the corresponding Collective Correction Note for ST-PI has been implemented.
Thanks.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Re: Email + Web Channel Translations via API?
entry.content:
Hey!

Technically I would say: "Yes it is possible". I did not test it myself yet but at least from what I can tell from the API documentation it should work

For it to work you would need to use the "Sections" API.

Basically going through your template based email with the List Sections endpoint:
https://dev.emarsys.com/docs/core-api-reference/tfyngxa5gtqz6-list-sections-in-a-template-based-email-campaign
Get the sections you need via the get sections endpoint
https://dev.emarsys.com/docs/core-api-reference/abbwhrwftt8sz-get-a-section-of-a-template-based-email-campaign
And then updating the sections via the update endpoint
https://dev.emarsys.com/docs/core-api-reference/qliyj4jesj5tj-update-a-section-of-a-template-based-email-campaign

 

To make it work properly, I would use something like a <span> tag that "mark" the parts you want to translate.

The issue I see with this method is, that I don't see a way of using the multi language feature. So you would need to copy the email campaign and then proceed with the steps mentioned above.

As said, I did not test it out myself but this is the approach I would take.

entry.title: Re: Payment strategy Payment API
entry.content:
Good day @jesseho271 

Thank you for your question.

No, there is currently no standard released API in SAP_COM_0727 that can be used to perform the same action as the Pay button in the payment strategy. It supports the payment requisition integration lifecycle, but it does not provide a released API operation equivalent to the Pay button in the payment strategy. You must carry out the actual payment execution through the standard Manage Payment Requisition- China app.

As a next step, i suggest you use SAP_COM_0727 to create and manage payment requisitions, then use the standard app for payment execution. If the Pay action fails, ensure you check the payment strategy logs and validate the payment method, house bank, bank account, BP bank data, special G/L indicator, and automatic payment related configuration you may have in your system. If you require external background execution of the Pay action, you should consider raising an enhancement request with SAP.

Here are references for more guidance

3478798 - The status is "Payment in Exceptions" in the app "Manage Payment Requisitions" - SAP for Me

3478684 - Error "No Valid Payment Method Found" occurs in the app "Manage Payment Requisitions" - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Data migrations ECC6-S4HANA
entry.content: It works in the short term, but it’s not a safe long-term strategy. In S/4HANA, Business Partner (BP) is the real master and BP is mandatory core object. Even if users create customers/vendors via: XD01 / XK01.  SAP still creates/updates a Business Partner in the background via CVI (Customer-Vendor Integration).

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026, top agenda picks for analytics practitioners
entry.content:
If you’ve ever walked into SAP Sapphire thinking "I’ll just play it by ear," you already know how that ends: many sessions competing for your attention, too many great hallway conversations, and a schedule that turns into a game of Tetris. Andrew Cabanski‑Dunning put it perfectly here: Sapphire rewards intention, define what success looks like for you, and plan around that.

For the Data Professionals community, I’m recommending 3 sessions that align with 2 practitioner-friendly goals: First, get an update directly from SAP Product Management leaders on the latest developments in analytics and AI within SAP Business Data Cloud, with a particular focus on the move toward agentic analytics and insight-to-action workflows. Second, hear firsthand from customers who have tackled the challenging work of standardizing metrics, operationalizing analytics, and creating an AI-ready foundation that can withstand the demands of governance and scale.

Together these sessions cover the full loop we all care about as analytics practitioners: ask questions in natural language without sacrificing business meaning, confidently explore governed metrics and semantic models, and take action by triggering simulations, write‑back, and workflow steps, all without leaving the analytics context.

Ask, explore, and act with AI-enabled analytics in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC2168)

This session is worth your 20 minutes. It is built around a simple, real‑life, premise: agentic analytics only works when the data foundation is context‑rich and governed. If the business logic, KPIs, and semantics aren’t consistent, your AI-enabled analytics experience won’t be either.

If you only attend one SAP‑led session, make it this one, because it calls out the baseline requirements many analytics practitioners are wrestling with right now:

  • Semantic modeling and reuse (define metrics once, trust them everywhere)
  • Self‑service composable models (build new answers from governed building blocks)
  • AI output governance (traceability, auditability, and humans‑in‑the‑loop)
  • Context‑aware conversational analytics (answers grounded in semantic + user context)
  • Insight‑to‑action (closing the loop by executing workflows in business applications)

Accelerate data and analytics with agentic AI in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC2745)

This session goes one step further into agentic experiences for data, analytics, and even planning. It describes a library of agent‑driven capabilities that practitioners can map to real workloads.

  • Automated data product creation via a conversational experience
  • Database exploration in natural language (for understanding objects)
  • Multi‑model data querying (structured + unstructured in one governed experience)
  • Insight generation into SAP Analytics Cloud stories and dashboards
  • Executive narratives and smart insights grounded in semantic models
  • Automated planning cycles driven by natural language prompts
  • Analytics and planning model generation with best practices built in

It also explicitly connects SAP Business Data Cloud capabilities (governance, semantics, knowledge graph, data products, analytics and simulations) to the end goal: a data fabric that connects data, context, and AI.

How Gilead standardizes planning, analytics and reporting for AI (BDC2163)

This is the customer story I’d prioritize because it’s the story many live through: forecast cycles, real‑time refresh pressure, governance needs, and the push to standardize before scaling AI.

Gilead will describe a planning and analytics journey aimed at reporting efficiency, clean actionable real‑time data, and explicitly laying foundation steps on the path to Generative AI. They also call out a very practical adoption pattern: Excel add‑in for self‑serve analytics plus SAP Analytics Cloud visualizations for OPEX, headcount, and revenue.

Join the Data Professionals community

If you’re heading to Orlando (or following along virtually), don’t let the learning end when the session doors close. The Data Professionals community is an open group where you can learn from experts, connect with peers, get the latest updates, and discover data and AI resources for SAP Business Data Cloud. Join here.

entry.title: Re: yaai & yaai_ui – Open-Source AI Tools for ABAP Developers
entry.content:
Hi @david_lincourt ,

Thanks a lot! I'm happy to hear you like it!

Regarding the API keys, you can find the documentation here:  https://github.com/christianjianelli/yaai/blob/main/docs/api_keys.md

The ABAP AI tools don’t provide a secure storage solution for API keys. For testing purposes, there is the class ycl_aai_api_key, which allows you to store your API key in the table yaai_api_key. 

Example: 

DATA(lo_key) = NEW ycl_aai_api_key( ).

" Create 
lo_key->create( i_id = 'OPENAI' i_api_key = 'sk-...' ).

Regarding the popup in YAAI_COCKPIT, could you please check if the ICF service yaai is active? 
You can verify this in transaction SICF. The authentication issue you described is probably related to this service. 

Thanks a lot.

Christian

entry.title: How to Call Dynamic Function in Sap ABAP
entry.content:
Data: LT_Ptab type ABAP_Func_parmbind_Tab,

lt_etab type ABAP_Func_excpbind_Tab,

lv_func_name type rs38l_fnam.

 

call function lv_func_name

parameter-table lt_ptab

exception-table lt_etab.

entry.title: Enhanced WorkFlow Notifications (Pending Data) - SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Introduction :

The Workflows always missed the necessary context in the Header and needed to be expanded or navigated through the link to look into the content. Workflow approvers always wanted critical data in the email notification , to decide on the priority to act on the workflow. This was a ask from Business for a while now, which was not feasible until now. Thanks to 1H 2026 Release , with the new release , Pending Data can now be mapped to the fields in the Document Generation Template and critical data can now be made visible in the email notification.

Enhanced Workflow Notifications :

The Object used here for the Configuration is Benefit Employee Claim which was the most asked from the Businesses.

Step 1 : Create a Document Generation Group

Document Generation - Manage Document Generation Template -> Create New -> Document Generation Group

Step 2 : Create a Document Generation Template

Document Generation - Manage Document Generation Template -> Create New -> Document Generation Template

Step 3 : Document Generation Template Mapping

The Critical Part of the configuration which does the Magic!

Notice the Reference (Pending Data) , As part of the Release SAP has introduced a new reference type called Pending Data which then allows you to select the Base Object and the field to be mapped.

Step 4 : Custom Workflow Configurations 

Custom Workflow Configuration is another critical aspect of the configuration.

Manage Workflow Email Configuration and Create a New Group 

Map the Document Generation Group to the Workflow Actions as per your Business Needs.

Step 5 : Assignment in Workflow

Assign the custom workflow under Workflow Email Configuration  

The Email Notification :

Raise a new Request (Reimbursement in this case) , and the Workflow Notification is sent with the most critical data required for the approvers to view and decide at a glance.

 

 

OLDEnhanced Workflow Notification
Create Benefit Employee Claim NotificationPurchase Claim Pending Approval

Notice the highlighted text in the Table and see how a Little context addition makes so much difference to the Workflow notification.

What used to be a usual typical workflow email notification is now enhanced with critical and necessary data. This is not limited to Benefit Employee Claim and can be used with any Custom MDF which would be the most used scenario of the Enhanced Workflow Notification.

Conclusion

What was once a basic workflow email notification is now transformed into a more informative and actionable communication. By surfacing critical data directly within the notification, approvers can make quicker and more informed decisions. This definitely will turn out as one of the most used features of the Employee Central.

entry.title: Re: convert date format YYYY.MM.DD to YYYYMMDD
entry.content: VDATU is not in a valid date format, so first convert it to string and remove delimiters using REPLACE, then convert it to DATE using TO_DATE with format YYYYMMDD.

entry.title: Introducing ATUL Architecture : A New Blog Series on SAP BTP for Intelligent Utilities
entry.content:
Over the past few years, I’ve had the chance to work closely with utilities that are modernizing their operations using SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, and event‑driven, real‑time architectures. One thing has become very clear: Utilities need simple, practical architecture patterns — not product brochures, not theory, and not marketing slides.

So I’m starting a new blog series called ATUL Architecture, where I’ll share practical patterns, diagrams, and lessons learned from real projects. The goal is simple: Make SAP BTP architecture easier to understand and easier to apply in the utilities industry.

This series is for architects, consultants, developers, and anyone working on SAP‑based utility transformation.

What the Series Will Cover

Here’s the plan for the first six posts:

ATUL Architecture #1 — Why Utilities Need a Digital Core on SAP BTP

  • Clean core

  • Integration strategy

  • Why BTP becomes the “digital backbone”

  • A simple digital‑core diagram

This post sets the foundation for the entire series.

 

ATUL Architecture #2 — Event‑Driven Utilities: Designing Real‑Time Flows on SAP BTP

  • Event Mesh

  • AMI → BTP → S/4HANA flow

  • Real‑time vs batch

  • A simple event‑flow diagram

This will focus on how utilities can move from slow batch processes to real‑time operations.

 

ATUL Architecture #3 — Bridging OT and IT: Bringing Grid Data into SAP BTP Safely

  • IEC 61850

  • MQTT

  • Substation → BTP patterns

  • Security considerations

This post will show how OT data can be brought into SAP BTP in a safe and scalable way.

 

ATUL Architecture #4 — AI on SAP BTP for Grid Operations

  • Time‑series data

  • Anomaly detection

  • Predictive maintenance

  • AI → workflow → S/4HANA integration

This will focus on how AI becomes actionable inside SAP processes.

 

ATUL Architecture #5 — Designing a Utility Data Fabric on SAP BTP

  • Data lake

  • Semantic models

  • Governance

  • Analytics for grid + customer

This post will cover how utilities can build a unified data layer on BTP.

 

ATUL Architecture #6 — Intelligent Utility Blueprint: End‑to‑End Architecture on SAP BTP

  • One consolidated diagram

  • End‑to‑end flow

  • Real‑time + AI + automation

  • Business outcomes

This will be the “big picture” post — the one that ties everything together.

I Want Your Input

Before I publish the first post, I’d love to hear from the SAP Community:

  • What topics would you like to see in this series?

  • Are there specific SAP BTP patterns you want explained?

  • Any challenges you’re facing in utilities, integration, or real‑time operations?

  • Should I include more diagrams, code snippets, or architecture trade‑offs?

Your suggestions will help shape the series.

What’s Next

I’ll publish ATUL Architecture #1 — Why Utilities Need a Digital Core on SAP BTP next. It will include a simple diagram and a practical explanation of why BTP becomes the digital backbone for modern utilities.

Looking forward to your ideas and feedback.

entry.title: Recovering UI5 adaptation project source from a deployed system
entry.content:
Introduction

UI5 adaptations can keep running in SAP long after the original developer and their local project are gone. This post explains why you cannot treat adaptations like ordinary BSP content, how adaptation files are actually stored, and how we automated downloading them from the layered repository into a local folder. You will learn which database table holds the data, what our small ABAP report does, and what you still need to set up by hand before using the files in your IDE.

The situation

Imagine you are asked to implement additional functionality for an already extended standard UI5 application. The extension is running in the system and users rely on it. The problem is that the team never used proper source code management, and the developer who built the adaptation has left the company—along with the local project files.

So the adaptation exists in SAP and the deployment is there. In theory you only need to “download it” again. In practice this is a bit awkward: there is no single, obvious option comparable to something like /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD that pulls the whole project. A main reason is that these adaptation files are not stored in the BSP repository; they live in the layered repository.

Inspecting files manually

You can inspect adaptation files one by one using the Layered Repository option in transaction SUI_SUPPORT (thanks to @abdullahgunes for pointing this out). 

You can view individual files, download them manually, and rebuild the folder structure yourself. That works, but it becomes tedious as soon as the adaptation has more than a handful of files.

An automated approach

We looked for a more automated way and ended up with a small ABAP report that reads UI5 adaptation files directly from the layered repository and writes them to a local folder on your machine.

The important technical detail: files in the layered repository are persisted in database table /UIF/LREPDCONT. That table holds what you need to reconstruct a project on disk: the namespace (including the project identifier and folder structure), each file’s name and type, and the content in binary form.

The report: ZR_LREP_ADAPTATION_DOWNLOAD

We created report ZR_LREP_ADAPTATION_DOWNLOAD and kept it deliberately simple. It has a single main input: the custom app (variant) ID. You can type it or use value help. When you run the report and choose a target folder, the program downloads all related files and recreates the original folder structure on your local machine.

Limitation: dist folder only

You should be aware of one limitation: at present, only the contents of the dist (which is created as a result of the build step) folder are stored in the layered repository in a way this approach recovers. This is enough to recreate the project as these are same with the contents of the webapp folder. You still need to create a dummy UI5 adaptation project in your IDE and then move the downloaded  content to the webapp folder into that project so you do not miss files such as ui5.yaml & package.json.

Reference implementation

The full source for the report is available here:

https://github.com/onurakca/SAPUI5-Adaptation-Project-Downloader

The report is provided free of charge under the MIT License. Use it at your own risk: test in non-production systems first, verify behavior in your landscape, and ensure compliance with your organization’s rules for custom ABAP and repository access.

Conclusion

This post walked through how UI5 adaptations are tied to the layered repository rather than classic BSP download flows, how table /UIF/LREPDCONT stores the files you need, and how ZR_LREP_ADAPTATION_DOWNLOAD bulk-exports them while preserving project structure. You now know the gap between “deployment exists in SAP” and “IDE-ready project,” including the webapp-only limitation and the step of dropping files into a new adaptation shell. Even with that limitation, this can save a lot of time when you inherit an adaptation with no Git history and need to review, compare, or extend it quickly.
